commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8373b8e40b7e0f54932b5b1a139f6152087a9081)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates cron payload normalization, merge logic, types, docs, and regression tests so `payload.model: null` or an empty string can clear an `agentTurn` model override on update.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +88, Docs +4. Total +115 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main deletes `payload.model: null` during normalization and only merges string model patches, while the live protocol schema also remains string-only. I did not run a live Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the branch is dirty, the live protocol/tool contract is incomplete, and automation cannot provide the contributor’s real Gateway proof.

target.title: fix(cron): allow clearing payload.model via update (fixes #91298)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The cron `update` API can't clear an `agentTurn` job's `payload.model`. `model: null` and `model: ""` are silently dropped, and omitting `model` merge-preserves the old value — so a hardcoded model stays pinned even after the agent's default model changes.
- **Why it matters:** Rotating your active model profile leaves existing cron jobs running on the old model. The only workarounds are bad: re-pin a current model (still hardcoded), or delete + recreate the job (loses `lastRunAtMs`, `consecutiveErrors`, `createdAtMs`, etc.).
- **What changed:** `payload.model: null` (or `""`) now clears the override so the job inherits the agent/global default — mirroring the existing nullable-clear pattern already used by `agentId`/`sessionKey`. The fix spans the four layers that each dropped the clear signal: protocol schema → patch type → normalization → merge.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Omitting `model` still preserves it (merge semantics unchanged). Create still never stores a `null` model. `payload.fallbacks` is intentionally untouched — it has no merge path at all today, so that's a separate issue, not part of this fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration (cron)
- [x] API / contracts

## Linked Issue

- Closes #91298

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`cron update` with `patch.payload.model = null` (or an empty string) now clears the model override and the job falls back to the agent/global default. A bare `{ payload: { model: null } }` patch is enough (it infers `agentTurn` kind). Omitting `model` preserves the current value. Documented in `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md`.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

1. Create an isolated `agentTurn` cron job with `payload.model` set to a specific model.
2. `cron update` the job with `patch: { payload: { model: null } }`.

### Expected

- `payload.model` is removed; the job inherits the agent/global default model.

### Actual (before this PR)

- `model: null` is dropped during normalization and the merge only applies string models, so the old model stays pinned.

## Evidence

- [x] **Failing test before + passing after.** Temporarily reverting the normalization + merge changes turns the 4 new behavioral tests red (`AssertionError: expected 'openrouter/some/model' to be undefined`); restoring the fix makes them green.
- 7 new regression tests across `src/cron/normalize.test.ts` and `src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts` (clear via `null`, clear via `""`, preserve on omit, update via string, create drops `null`, bare model-only clear infers `agentTurn`).
- Full local run (Node 22): 5 cron suites pass (115 tests) — `normalize`, `service.jobs`, `cron-protocol-conformance`, `cron-tool`, `gateway/server.cron`. Lint, format, and `tsgo` clean on all touched files.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: model cleared via `null`; cleared via `""`; preserved when the patch omits it; updated when a string is provided; create never stores a `null` model; a bare `{ payload: { model: null } }` patch merges (clears) instead of throwing "requires message".
- Edge cases checked: kind-switch payload rebuild coerces a cleared `null` model to "unset".
- Not verified: a live end-to-end gateway round-trip (verified at unit/source level only).

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — additive. `model: null` was previously rejected/ignored; valid existing patches behave identically.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## AI assistance

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Fully tested locally (unit + source-level). Author understands the change. Local `codex review` was unavailable in this environment; the GitHub Codex review on this PR will cover that pass.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a caller relying on the old behavior where `model: null` was a silent no-op.
  - Mitigation: that path returned an unchanged job (clearing was impossible), so no caller could depend on a "cleared" outcome; passing `null` now does the documented, expected thing.
target.author: ly-wang19
